fixes.origin: String query = "SELECT value FROM metrics WHERE name = '" + metric + "'";
fixes.replacement:
String safe = metric.matches("[a-zA-Z0-9_]+") ? metric : "cpu";
String query = "SELECT value FROM metrics WHERE name = '" + safe + "'";

if (!name.matches("[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+")) throw new IOException("invalid");
File f = new File(logDir + "/" + name);

fixes.origin:
String cmd = "sh -c " + script;

Process p = Runtime.getRuntime().exec(cmd);

static_findings.message: Possible SQL injection: SQL query variable "query" appears to be built unsafely before execution.
static_findings.evidence: String query = "SELECT value FROM metrics WHERE name = '" + metric + "'"

static_findings.message: Runtime.getRuntime().exec() may allow OS command injection if input is not controlled.

static_findings.vulnerability: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
static_findings.message: MessageDigest.getInstance("MD5") uses MD5, which is considered cryptographically weak.
